§6Oil Tar§f

§aA sticky byproduct with multiple variants — essential for crafting fluid tanks and high-capacity storage!§f

Oil Tar is a solid petroleum product produced as a byproduct from solidifying heavy oils or from fractioning cracked oil. It comes in §e5§f interchangeable variants: §bOil Tar§f, §bCrack Oil Tar§f, §bCoal Tar§f, §bWood Tar§f, and §bParaffin Wax§f. (Note: Wood Tar may not work in all recipes.)

§6Production§f:
§b• Industrial Solidification Machine§f: Solidify §bCrude Oil§f, §bBitumen§f, or §bHeavy Oil§f → §e1×§f Oil Tar.
§b• Oil Refinery§f (cracked oil variant): Processing §bCracked Oil§f produces §bCrack Oil Tar§f as a byproduct instead of sulfur.
§b• Industrial Liquefaction Machine§f: Produce §bCoal Oil§f → solidify into §bCoal Tar§f (easiest method for large quantities).
§b• Wood Oil§f solidification → §bWood Tar§f.
§b• Industrial Solidification§f (Engine Lubricant) → §bParaffin Wax§f.

§6Uses§f:
§b• Black Dye§f: Can be used as a substitute for black dye in crafting recipes.
§b• Fluid Tanks§f: Required for crafting §bSteel Barrels§f, §bUniversal Fluid Tanks§f, and §bBAT-9000§f high-capacity storage.
§b• Coking§f: Wood Tar can be processed in the §bCombination Oven§f to produce §bCoke§f (but cannot be acidized into Chlorinated Petroleum Wax).

§eTip§f: §bCoal Tar§f is the easiest to mass-produce. The §bIndustrial Liquefaction Machine§f itself needs tar to craft, so start with §bCrude Oil§f solidification for your first bits. §bParaffin Wax§f combines with §bChlorine Gas§f in the §bAcidizer§f for Chlorinated Petroleum Wax — a unique advanced material.
